rulings.Case Title: TODD V SENIGAR VS. UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 15, 2011, line 1. DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Deny motion for summary judgment. Deny motion for summary adjudication as to 4th cause of action (race discrimination). Grant motion for summary adjudication as to remaining causes of action (1, 2, 3, 5, 6 and 7). #1 - (wage discrimination) no evidence that any disparity in wage based race; #2 - retaliation - no evidence of retaliation by Fritz/defendant; #3 - failure prevent harassment - no evidence of harassment (dirty look, what need to do get promotion be black) need severe and pervasive conduct that alters work conditions; #5 - failure to accomodate - not mentioned in E.E.O.C. 1-3 charges failed exhaust administrative remedies Title VII; #6 (race discrimination) and #7 (retaliation) - time barred by 90 days. =(302/PHA)
